description:
You are a FinOps analyst inside a Linux shell.  
Your goal is to set up, standardise, and document a family of symbolic links that point to the most recent cloud-cost CSV files so that downstream scripts can always find “latest” data without having to know the exact file name.

The filesystem currently contains:  
/home/user/cloud_costs/monthly/  
├── cost_2023-04.csv  
├── cost_2023-05.csv  
├── cost_2023-06.csv  
└── cost_2023-07.csv  

Nothing else exists inside /home/user/cloud_costs/ at the start.

1. Create **two** new symbolic links inside a freshly-created directory /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/  
   1. latest.csv  →  ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  
   2. previous.csv →  ../monthly/cost_2023-06.csv  
   •  The links **must use these exact relative target strings** shown above (do NOT automatically expand them to absolute paths).

2. Create a directory /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/ and inside it create three symbolic links that all point to the newest file:  
   • compute_latest.csv  →  ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  
   • storage_latest.csv  →  ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  
   • network_latest.csv  →  ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  

   Again, the links have to be relative and use the exact target text shown.

3. Produce an audit file at /home/user/cloud_costs/link_audit.log that enumerates every link you created.  
   Output format (one line per link, **exactly**):  
   SYMLINK_PATH -> TARGET_PATH  
   Example (do not include the example in your file):  
   /path/to/link -> ../target/file  

   Required order of the five lines in link_audit.log:  
   a) /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/latest.csv   -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  
   b) /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/previous.csv -> ../monthly/cost_2023-06.csv  
   c) /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/compute_latest.csv  -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  
   d) /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/storage_latest.csv  -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  
   e) /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/network_latest.csv  -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  

   The automated tests will fail if:  
   •  Any path or arrow “->” token deviates (including extra spaces).  
   •  The file contains more or fewer than five lines.  
   •  The links are not present or do not resolve to the correct targets when readlink ‑f is used.

No root privileges are available, so keep all work under /home/user.  
When you are done, verify your work by listing the links and showing the contents of link_audit.log; console output is allowed and encouraged.
truth:
Initial directory tree and file contents that exist before the agent starts:

Directory: /home/user/cloud_costs/monthly/

File: /home/user/cloud_costs/monthly/cost_2023-04.csv  
Contents (including trailing newline):
Date,Service,CostUSD
2023-04-30,Compute,123.45
2023-04-30,Storage,67.89
2023-04-30,Network,10.11

File: /home/user/cloud_costs/monthly/cost_2023-05.csv  
Contents:
Date,Service,CostUSD
2023-05-31,Compute,150.12
2023-05-31,Storage,70.55
2023-05-31,Network,12.34

File: /home/user/cloud_costs/monthly/cost_2023-06.csv  
Contents:
Date,Service,CostUSD
2023-06-30,Compute,131.07
2023-06-30,Storage,72.88
2023-06-30,Network,11.98

File: /home/user/cloud_costs/monthly/cost_2023-07.csv  
Contents:
Date,Service,CostUSD
2023-07-31,Compute,142.33
2023-07-31,Storage,75.66
2023-07-31,Network,13.55

Expected artefacts after the agent completes the task:

1. Directory /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/ exists (permissions 755 or default).
2. Symlink /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/latest.csv   → ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv (link string exactly as shown).
3. Symlink /home/user/cloud_costs/processed/previous.csv → ../monthly/cost_2023-06.csv (exact string).
4. Directory /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/ exists.
5. Symlink /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/compute_latest.csv  → ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv
6. Symlink /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/storage_latest.csv  → ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv
7. Symlink /home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/network_latest.csv  → ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv
8. File /home/user/cloud_costs/link_audit.log exists with EXACT contents (including final newline):
/home/user/cloud_costs/processed/latest.csv -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv
/home/user/cloud_costs/processed/previous.csv -> ../monthly/cost_2023-06.csv
/home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/compute_latest.csv -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv
/home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/storage_latest.csv -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv
/home/user/cloud_costs/per_service/network_latest.csv -> ../monthly/cost_2023-07.csv

Trailing newline after the last line is allowed but not required.

Automated verification steps (implicit, not visible to the agent):
• readlink -n on each symlink to ensure the stored path matches exactly the strings above.  
• readlink -f to ensure each points to an existing CSV file.  
• diff between link_audit.log and the expected 5-line blob above.
